title: erythronium oregonum subsp. leucandrum
intro: Erythronium oregonum subsp. leucandrum, also known as the white fawn lily or white dogtooth violet, is a species of flowering plant in the lily family that is native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce pain. The health benefits of Erythronium oregonum subsp. leucandrum include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant include decoctions, infusions, and salves, which can be made by steeping the roots or flowers in hot water or oil to release their medicinal compounds.
